title: Cat Vomiting White Foam Continuously: Urgency and Care Guide
meta_desc: If your cat is vomiting white foam continuously, it may be a medical emergency. Learn why immediate veterinary care and triage photos are critical.
content_html:
What is Continuous White Foam Vomiting?

When a cat vomits white foam, it typically means they are bringing up gastric juices and saliva from an empty stomach. While an occasional episode might be related to a hairball, continuous or repetitive vomiting indicates that the stomach lining is severely irritated or that there is an underlying systemic issue that needs to be addressed immediately.

Urgency Level: High Emergency

If your cat is vomiting white foam continuously, this is considered a high-priority emergency. Continuous vomiting quickly leads to severe dehydration, electrolyte imbalances, and physical exhaustion. This can be caused by life-threatening conditions such as intestinal blockages, ingestion of toxins, kidney disease, or severe pancreatitis. You should contact your nearest emergency veterinarian immediately.

Why Taking a Photo Helps Triage

Capturing a quick photo of the vomit can be incredibly helpful for the veterinary team during triage. A photo allows the veterinarian to see the exact consistency, volume, and color of the foam. This visual evidence helps them distinguish between simple gastric upset and signs of more serious issues, such as the presence of flecks of blood or bile, which can accelerate the diagnostic process and prioritize your cat's care.

faqs.question: Should I withhold food and water?
faqs.answer: Yes. If your cat is vomiting continuously, do not offer food or water as it will likely trigger more vomiting and worsen dehydration. Seek veterinary care immediately.

faqs.question: Can I give my cat over-the-counter stomach medicine?
faqs.answer: No. Never give human medications to a cat without a veterinarian's explicit instruction, as many are toxic and can complicate the underlying issue.

faqs.question: What if my cat is also lethargic?
faqs.answer: Lethargy combined with continuous vomiting is a sign of a critical condition. This suggests the body is struggling to compensate and requires urgent medical intervention.
